role_title: Associate, New Verticals - Convenience Strategy & Operations

location.office_time_required: a few days a month in person

level_signals: Base range $74,800–$110,000 USD consistent with junior-to-mid IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 1-3+ years of experience. You've succeeded in similar cultures that are highly analytical and cross-functional (whether in tech, operations, strategy, consulting, banking, or a related experience).
you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL and building models in excel.
Build models using Excel and SQL to evaluate the economic, operational and strategic costs and benefits of various programs and experiments
You're analytical. You let data win arguments, and you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL and building models in excel.
You solve problems from first principles. You have a talent for synthesizing complex problems, can translate high-level goals into actionable plans, and enjoy implementing a process
You are an owner. You're always looking for opportunities to better your work product and own the outcome – both the good and the bad
You're entrepreneurial. You have a bias for action and are able to build things from the ground up.
You're unafraid of ambiguity. Rapid change and learning is exciting to you, and you thrive in ambiguous, startup environment
You're energized by AI tools — and you actively experiment with them. You don't wait to be told how to use a new tool; you're already exploring tools like Claude, Cursor, Gemini and more.
You have a completed Bachelor's Degree.
Lead, collaborate and Influence. You'll partner with and influence cross-functional teams including growth, quality, finance, product and engineering to launch initiatives to the finish line.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You bring a builder's mindset to AI: using it to automate the repetitive, accelerate the analytical, and unlock work that would otherwise require a larger team.
You'll use AI tools to automate manual processes, build new prototypes and develop product visions.

scope.surface: Convenience vertical within DoorDash New Verticals — enterprise merchant growth, quality/operational improvement, and consumer experience
core_responsibilities: Identifying, validating, and launching high-impact '0-to-1' initiatives that unlock new growth opportunities across the convenience merchant base
Partnering with cross-functional teams to enhance in-app merchandising and optimize the customer journey across the funnel
Developing initiatives that strengthen DoorDash's position as the preferred destination for convenience shopping through improved selection, discoverability, and quality
Leading operational improvement projects focused on increasing order accuracy, fulfillment speed, and overall customer satisfaction
Streamlining in-store fulfillment processes to improve efficiency and create a seamless experience for Dashers and merchant partners
Developing and executing on programs that improve inventory accuracy and ensure real-time platform availability reflects in-store stock levels
Own tracking dashboards and manage week over week metrics to drive high quality operations
Build models using Excel and SQL to evaluate the economic, operational and strategic costs and benefits of various programs and experiments
Use AI tools to automate manual processes, build new prototypes and develop product visions

green_flags: Two open positions — candidate considered for both, improving odds of fit
Clear description of two distinct role tracks (growth vs. operational excellence)
Explicit AI tool usage expected, signaling a forward-looking team culture
Profitable, high-MAU vertical described — suggests team has resources and visibility
Hybrid with low in-person requirement ('a few days a month') — flexible for most candidates
red_flags: Role alignment (growth vs. quality track) determined post-interview by hiring panel — candidate has limited control over which role they land in
Compensation range is relatively wide ($35K spread), introducing uncertainty about where a candidate would land
No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Cities not listed for hybrid office requirement — unclear which office(s) apply

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Associate', years range 1-3+, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as KPIs)
location.cities_listed (not specified in JD body for this role)
ambiguities: JD says '1-3+ years of experience' — the '+' after '3' is unusual and could imply openness to more experienced candidates; treated as a 1–3 year floor with upside flexibility.

Two distinct roles are described (growth-focused vs. quality/operations-focused) under a single posting — role alignment determined post-interview, which is atypical and may affect candidate expectations.

AI tool usage expectations (Claude, Cursor, Gemini, automation) appear in both the 'must-have' qualifications section and the responsibilities section, making it ambiguous whether this is a hard requirement or a strong preference; classified as hard given unconditional language in the qualifications block.
